title: Linux Patches Introduce "KNOD" For In-Kernel Network Offloading Directly To AMD GPUs
byline: Written by Michael Larabel in Linux Networking on 20 July 2026 at 09:38 AM EDT. 25 Comments
excerpt: Some extremely cool patches were posted to the Linux kernel mailing list on Sunday
content_html:
Some extremely cool patches were posted to the Linux kernel mailing list on Sunday. The patches for "KNOD" allow for in-kernel network offloading to GPUs with an initial focus on AMD GPU support. What makes this all the more nifty is that it doesn't depend upon any user-space libraries like AMD ROCm but is all handled in-kernel with driving the GPU directly.

After initially presenting his concept last year at the Linux Plumbers Conference in Japan, Taehee Yoo yesterday posted the KNOD patches to the Linux kernel mailing list under a Request For Comments (RFC) banner.

KNOD is for in-kernel network offload device that is entirely kernel driven for accelerated packet processing without the need for ROCm or any other user-space components. The kernel code itself is managing the GPU queues, JIT compiling the per-packet program to GPU machine code, and dispatching the work.The network adapter DMAs the received packets straight into the GPU memory. The concept also allows for offloading existing XDP programs and IPsec SAs directly to the GPU in a transparent manner.

"Line-rate packet processing that does non-trivial per-packet work - an XDP program doing L4 load balancing, or IPsec crypto - is bound by the host CPU: each core handles one packet at a time, so scaling means spending more cores. A GPU is the opposite shape - thousands of lanes running the same small program over many packets at once (SIMT) - which happens to match the per-packet-program model of XDP.

knod moves that per-packet compute off the host CPU and onto a GPU. The NIC DMAs received packets directly into GPU memory, the GPU runs the program across a batch of packets in parallel, and only the result comes back: a verdict for every packet, plus the packet itself for the ones destined to the host. The CPU no longer pays the per-packet program cost, and throughput scales with GPU occupancy rather than core
count.

Crucially this happens entirely inside the kernel. GPU packet processing today generally launches work from a userspace GPU runtime (CUDA and friends) and keeps that runtime in the data path; knod instead builds the GPU queues, compiles the program, and dispatches it from the kernel, so it plugs into existing offload paths (XDP, xfrm) with nothing to install or keep running in userspace."

Development of this code happened on GCN hardware with the Radeon RX Vega as well as on RDNA2 hardware. AMD has the distinct advantage here due to the fully open-source and upstream driver stack. On the NVIDIA side there is their official open-source kernel driver but not upstream and then the Nouveau/NOVA drivers not yet being mature enough for taking on such work. Supporting Intel GPUs with KNOD may end up being an option too but for now all the work has been done with AMD GPUs.

These very exciting Linux kernel patches for KNOD can be reviewed on the Linux kernel mailing list.

More background information on this in-kernel network packet processing using GPUs directly can be found via the LPC 2025 presentation.

sentences.zh: 周日，一些非常酷的补丁被发布到 Linux 内核邮件列表中。

sentences.en: The patches for "KNOD" allow for in-kernel network offloading to GPUs with an initial focus on AMD GPU support.
sentences.zh: “KNOD”补丁允许内核内网络卸载到 GPU，最初重点关注 AMD GPU 支持。

sentences.en: What makes this all the more nifty is that it doesn't depend upon any user-space libraries like AMD ROCm but is all handled in-kernel with driving the GPU directly.
sentences.zh: 更妙的是，它不依赖于 AMD ROCm 等任何用户空间库，而是在内核中直接驱动 GPU 进行处理。

sentences.en: After initially presenting his concept last year at the Linux Plumbers Conference in Japan, Taehee Yoo yesterday posted the KNOD patches to the Linux kernel mailing list under a Request For Comments (RFC) banner.

sentences.en: KNOD is for in-kernel network offload device that is entirely kernel driven for accelerated packet processing without the need for ROCm or any other user-space components.
sentences.zh: KNOD 适用于内核网络卸载设备，完全由内核驱动，可加速数据包处理，无需 ROCm 或任何其他用户空间组件。

sentences.en: The kernel code itself is managing the GPU queues, JIT compiling the per-packet program to GPU machine code, and dispatching the work.The network adapter DMAs the received packets straight into the GPU memory.
sentences.zh: 内核代码本身管理 GPU 队列，将每个数据包的程序 JIT 编译为 GPU 机器代码，并调度工作。网络适配器将接收到的数据包直接 DMA 到 GPU 内存中。

sentences.en: The concept also allows for offloading existing XDP programs and IPsec SAs directly to the GPU in a transparent manner.
sentences.zh: 该概念还允许以透明的方式将现有的 XDP 程序和 IPsec SA 直接卸载到 GPU。

blocks.html_en:
"Line-rate packet processing that does non-trivial per-packet work - an XDP program doing L4 load balancing, or IPsec crypto - is bound by the host CPU: each core handles one packet at a time, so scaling means spending more cores. A GPU is the opposite shape - thousands of lanes running the same small program over many packets at once (SIMT) - which happens to match the per-packet-program model of XDP.

knod moves that per-packet compute off the host CPU and onto a GPU. The NIC DMAs received packets directly into GPU memory, the GPU runs the program across a batch of packets in parallel, and only the result comes back: a verdict for every packet, plus the packet itself for the ones destined to the host. The CPU no longer pays the per-packet program cost, and throughput scales with GPU occupancy rather than core
count.

Crucially this happens entirely inside the kernel. GPU packet processing today generally launches work from a userspace GPU runtime (CUDA and friends) and keeps that runtime in the data path; knod instead builds the GPU queues, compiles the program, and dispatches it from the kernel, so it plugs into existing offload paths (XDP, xfrm) with nothing to install or keep running in userspace."

sentences.en: "Line-rate packet processing that does non-trivial per-packet work - an XDP program doing L4 load balancing, or IPsec crypto - is bound by the host CPU: each core handles one packet at a time, so scaling means spending more cores.
sentences.zh: “对每个数据包进行重要工作的线速数据包处理（执行 L4 负载平衡或 IPsec 加密的 XDP 程序）受到主机 CPU 的约束：每个核心一次处理一个数据包，因此扩展意味着花费更多核心。

sentences.en: A GPU is the opposite shape - thousands of lanes running the same small program over many packets at once (SIMT) - which happens to match the per-packet-program model of XDP.
sentences.zh: GPU 是相反的形状 - 数千个通道同时在多个数据包上运行相同的小程序 (SIMT) - 这恰好与 XDP 的每数据包程序模型相匹配。

sentences.en: knod moves that per-packet compute off the host CPU and onto a GPU.
sentences.zh: knod 将每个数据包的计算从主机 CPU 转移到 GPU 上。

sentences.en: The NIC DMAs received packets directly into GPU memory, the GPU runs the program across a batch of packets in parallel, and only the result comes back: a verdict for every packet, plus the packet itself for the ones destined to the host.
sentences.zh: NIC DMA 将数据包直接接收到 GPU 内存中，GPU 在一批数据包上并行运行程序，并且只返回结果：每个数据包的判决，以及发往主机的数据包本身。

sentences.en: The CPU no longer pays the per-packet program cost, and throughput scales with GPU occupancy rather than core count.
sentences.zh: CPU 不再支付每个数据包的程序成本，并且吞吐量随 GPU 占用而不是核心数量而变化。

sentences.en: Crucially this happens entirely inside the kernel.
sentences.zh: 至关重要的是，这完全发生在内核内部。

sentences.en: GPU packet processing today generally launches work from a userspace GPU runtime (CUDA and friends) and keeps that runtime in the data path; knod instead builds the GPU queues, compiles the program, and dispatches it from the kernel, so it plugs into existing offload paths (XDP, xfrm) with nothing to install or keep running in userspace."
sentences.zh: 如今，GPU 数据包处理通常从用户空间 GPU 运行时（CUDA 等）启动工作，并将该运行时保留在数据路径中；相反，knod 构建 GPU 队列、编译程序并从内核分派它，因此它可以插入现有的卸载路径（XDP、xfrm），无需安装任何内容或在用户空间中保持运行。”

count.

sentences.zh: 有关直接使用 GPU 进行内核网络数据包处理的更多背景信息，请参阅 LPC 2025 演示文稿。

content_html_zh:
周日，一些非常酷的补丁被发布到 Linux 内核邮件列表中。 “KNOD”补丁允许内核内网络卸载到 GPU，最初重点关注 AMD GPU 支持。 更妙的是，它不依赖于 AMD ROCm 等任何用户空间库，而是在内核中直接驱动 GPU 进行处理。

去年在日本举行的 Linux Plumbers Conference 上首次提出了他的概念后，Taehee Yoo 昨天在征求意见 (RFC) 的旗帜下将 KNOD 补丁发布到了 Linux 内核邮件列表中。

KNOD 适用于内核网络卸载设备，完全由内核驱动，可加速数据包处理，无需 ROCm 或任何其他用户空间组件。 内核代码本身管理 GPU 队列，将每个数据包的程序 JIT 编译为 GPU 机器代码，并调度工作。网络适配器将接收到的数据包直接 DMA 到 GPU 内存中。 该概念还允许以透明的方式将现有的 XDP 程序和 IPsec SA 直接卸载到 GPU。

“对每个数据包进行重要工作的线速数据包处理（执行 L4 负载平衡或 IPsec 加密的 XDP 程序）受到主机 CPU 的约束：每个核心一次处理一个数据包，因此扩展意味着花费更多核心。 GPU 是相反的形状 - 数千个通道同时在多个数据包上运行相同的小程序 (SIMT) - 这恰好与 XDP 的每数据包程序模型相匹配。 knod 将每个数据包的计算从主机 CPU 转移到 GPU 上。 NIC DMA 将数据包直接接收到 GPU 内存中，GPU 在一批数据包上并行运行程序，并且只返回结果：每个数据包的判决，以及发往主机的数据包本身。 CPU 不再支付每个数据包的程序成本，并且吞吐量随 GPU 占用而不是核心数量而变化。 至关重要的是，这完全发生在内核内部。 如今，GPU 数据包处理通常从用户空间 GPU 运行时（CUDA 等）启动工作，并将该运行时保留在数据路径中；相反，knod 构建 GPU 队列、编译程序并从内核分派它，因此它可以插入现有的卸载路径（XDP、xfrm），无需安装任何内容或在用户空间中保持运行。”

此代码的开发发生在带有 Radeon RX Vega 的 GCN 硬件以及 RDNA2 硬件上。 由于完全开源和上游驱动程序堆栈，AMD 在这方面具有明显的优势。 在 NVIDIA 方面，有他们的官方开源内核驱动程序，但没有上游，而且 Nouveau/NOVA 驱动程序还不够成熟，无法承担此类工作。 通过 KNOD 支持 Intel GPU 也可能最终成为一种选择，但目前所有工作都已通过 AMD GPU 完成。

这些非常令人兴奋的 KNOD Linux 内核补丁可以在 Linux 内核邮件列表中查看。

有关直接使用 GPU 进行内核网络数据包处理的更多背景信息，请参阅 LPC 2025 演示文稿。
